The **2008–09 Segunda División** season (known from that season as [Liga Adelante](/wiki/Liga_Adelante "Liga Adelante") for sponsorship reasons) was the 78th since its establishment. The first matches of the season were played on 30 August 2008, and the season ended on 21 June 2009. [Real Zaragoza](/wiki/Real_Zaragoza "Real Zaragoza"), [Real Murcia](/wiki/Real_Murcia "Real Murcia") and [Levante](/wiki/Levante_UD "Levante UD") were the teams relegated from [La Liga](/wiki/La_Liga "La Liga") the previous season. [Alicante](/wiki/Alicante_CF "Alicante CF"), [Girona](/wiki/Girona_FC "Girona FC"), [Huesca](/wiki/SD_Huesca "SD Huesca") and [Rayo Vallecano](/wiki/Rayo_Vallecano "Rayo Vallecano") were the teams promoted from [Segunda División B](/wiki/Segunda_Divisi%C3%B3n_B "Segunda División B") the previous season.

The first goal of the season was scored by [Alexandre Geijo](/wiki/Alexandre_Geijo "Alexandre Geijo"), who scored in the 11th minute for Levante against [Real Zaragoza](/wiki/Real_Zaragoza "Real Zaragoza") in the early kick-off. The first red card of the season was given to [Paco Peña](/wiki/Francisco_Pe%C3%B1a_Romero "Francisco Peña Romero") from Real Murcia after a challenge on [Sergio Pachón](/wiki/Sergio_Pach%C3%B3n "Sergio Pachón") in their opening match against [Rayo Vallecano](/wiki/Rayo_Vallecano "Rayo Vallecano"). The first [hat-trick](/wiki/Hat-trick "Hat-trick") was scored by [Sebastián Abreu](/wiki/Sebasti%C3%A1n_Abreu "Sebastián Abreu") in the match between Xerez and [Real Sociedad](/wiki/Real_Sociedad "Real Sociedad").
